  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda (v4.5+)
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Copy environment.yaml and requirements.txt if not present (optional step, in case they're missing)
  # If you have custom environment.yaml and requirements.txt, add them in Docker build context.
  # Otherwise, use the ones from repo.
  # Create conda environment
  RUN conda env create --file environment.yaml || \
      (conda create -y -n u-sleep python=3.9 && \
       conda activate u-sleep && \
       pip install -r requirements.txt)
  # Activate environment for subsequent RUN commands
  SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
  ENV PATH /opt/conda/envs/u-sleep/bin:$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  ENV CONDA_DEFAULT_ENV u-sleep
  # Install U-Time package (setup.py/pip)
  RUN conda activate u-sleep && \
      pip install . && \
      python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available and install extras
  RUN conda activate u-sleep && \
      ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Default to bash shell in repo root
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Install pip requirements as a fallback for anything missed in environment.yaml
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install -r requirements.txt
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install .
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 2 ---

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install pip requirements with output logging for troubleshooting
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install -r requirements.txt > /tmp/pip_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_install.log && exit 1)
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install pip requirements, outputting log even on failure for diagnostics
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install -r requirements.txt > /tmp/pip_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_install.log && false)
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Split pip install for easier diagnostics and error handling
  RUN while read req; do \
        if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
          echo "Installing: $req"; \
          conda run -n u-sleep pip install "$req" >> /tmp/pip_install_split.log 2>&1 || (echo "FAILED: $req" && tail -n 20 /tmp/pip_install_split.log && exit 1); \
        fi; \
      done < requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install_split.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install_split.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Split pip install for easier diagnostics and error handling, with improved diagnostics
  RUN set -x; \
      while read req; do \
        if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
          echo "Installing: $req"; \
          conda run -n u-sleep pip install "$req" >> /tmp/pip_install_split.log 2>&1 || ( \
            echo "FAILED: $req"; \
            echo "=== Last 20 lines of /tmp/pip_install_split.log ==="; \
            tail -n 20 /tmp/pip_install_split.log; \
            exit 1 \
          ); \
        fi; \
      done < requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install_split.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install_split.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 6 ---

  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

      ```dockerfile
      RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
          while read req; do \
              if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
                  echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
              fi; \
          done < requirements.txt; \
          echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
      ```

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Print requirements.txt contents line by line for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ===" && \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt && \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="
  # Split pip install for easier diagnostics, printing error context
  RUN set -e; \
      while read req; do \
        if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
          echo "Installing: $req"; \
          conda run -n u-sleep pip install "$req" >> /tmp/pip_install_split.log 2>&1 || ( \
            echo "FAILED: $req"; \
            echo "=== Last 40 lines of /tmp/pip_install_split.log ==="; \
            tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_install_split.log; \
            exit 1 \
          ); \
        fi; \
      done < requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install_split.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install_split.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 7 ---

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Print requirements.txt contents line by line for diagnostics using bash
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Split pip install for easier diagnostics, printing error context
  RUN set -e; \
      while read req; do \
        if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
          echo "Installing: $req"; \
          conda run -n u-sleep pip install "$req" >> /tmp/pip_install_split.log 2>&1 || ( \
            echo "FAILED: $req"; \
            echo "=== Last 40 lines of /tmp/pip_install_split.log ==="; \
            tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_install_split.log; \
            exit 1 \
          ); \
        fi; \
      done < requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install_split.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install_split.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.
  ---
  **Summary:**  
  This adjusted plan ensures the environment setup is fully compatible with a CPU-only, x86_64 Linux system, omitting any GPU/CUDA steps or dependencies, and is ready for use in Docker or bare-metal environments with your provided hardware specs.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Print requirements.txt contents line by line for diagnostics using bash
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Split pip install for easier diagnostics, printing error context, using --no-cache-dir and verbose mode
  RUN set -e; \
      while read req; do \
        if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
          echo "Installing: $req"; \
          conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir -v "$req" >> /tmp/pip_install_split.log 2>&1 || ( \
            echo "FAILED: $req"; \
            echo "=== Last 80 lines of /tmp/pip_install_split.log ==="; \
            tail -n 80 /tmp/pip_install_split.log; \
            exit 1 \
          ); \
        fi; \
      done < requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install_split.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install_split.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 9 ---

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Print requirements.txt contents line by line for diagnostics using bash
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Per recommendations: skip pip install for requirements already satisfied by conda
  # Only install via pip requirements not in conda list (diagnostic), continue on error but report package
  RUN set -e; \
      conda run -n u-sleep conda list | awk '{print $1}' > /tmp/_conda_pkgs.txt; \
      touch /tmp/pip_install_split.log; \
      while read req; do \
        if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
          pkg=$(echo "$req" | cut -d= -f1 | cut -d[ -f1 | sed 's/ *$//g'); \
          if grep -i -Fxq "$pkg" /tmp/_conda_pkgs.txt; then \
            echo "SKIPPING (in conda): $req"; \
          else \
            echo "Installing: $req"; \
            conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir -v "$req" >> /tmp/pip_install_split.log 2>&1 || ( \
              echo "FAILED: $req"; \
              echo "=== Last 80 lines of /tmp/pip_install_split.log ==="; \
              tail -n 80 /tmp/pip_install_split.log; \
              exit 1 \
            ); \
          fi; \
        fi; \
      done < requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_install_split.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_install_split.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 10 ---

  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Print requirements.txt contents line by line for diagnostics using bash
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Attempt pip install all requirements at once (bulk), output full log on failure
  RUN set -e; \
      conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt > /tmp/pip_bulk_install.log 2>&1 || ( \
        echo "=== pip bulk install failed ==="; \
        tail -n 80 /tmp/pip_bulk_install.log; \
        exit 1 \
      )
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_bulk_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_bulk_install.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Install U-Time package
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 12 ---

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools and wheel are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
  # Install U-Time package, and show pip install log if it fails
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install setuptools-git explicitly in case it's required by setup.py
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3"
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools and wheel are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
  # Install U-Time package, and show pip install log if it fails
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install . > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install setuptools-git and wheel explicitly to support setup.py
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3" wheel
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools and wheel are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
  # Install U-Time package, and show pip install log if it fails
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel > /tmp/pip_utime_build.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log && exit 1)
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install dist/*.whl > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install setuptools-git and wheel explicitly to support setup.py
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3" wheel
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools and wheel are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
  # Diagnostic: check all packaging files exist before wheel build
  RUN ls -l README.md HISTORY.rst LICENSE.txt utime/version.py
  # Install U-Time package, and show pip install log if it fails
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel > /tmp/pip_utime_build.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log && exit 1)
  RUN test -f dist/*.whl && conda run -n u-sleep pip install dist/*.whl > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 16 ---

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install setuptools-git and wheel explicitly to support setup.py
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3" wheel
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools and wheel are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
  # Diagnostic: check all packaging files exist before wheel build
  RUN ls -l README.md HISTORY.rst LICENSE.txt utime/version.py
  # Early check for packaging errors
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py check > /tmp/pip_utime_check.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_check.log && exit 1)
  # Install U-Time package, and show pip install log if it fails
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel > /tmp/pip_utime_build.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic: list contents of dist directory
  RUN echo "=== dist directory contents ===" && ls -l dist || true
  # Fail early and show build log if wheel is missing
  RUN if [ ! -e dist/*.whl ]; then echo "=== dist/*.whl missing ==="; cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log; exit 1; fi
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install dist/*.whl > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  **Additional Platform-Specific Notes:**
  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).
  - **Paths:** All paths in scripts, Dockerfiles, and documentation should reference the actual directory structure as per your Working Directory.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install setuptools-git and wheel explicitly to support setup.py
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3" wheel
  # Diagnostic: show requirements.txt contents line by line
  RUN bash -c 'echo "=== BEGIN per-requirement pip install debug ==="; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "WILL INSTALL: $req"; \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt; \
      echo "=== END per-requirement pip install debug ==="'
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools and wheel are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel
  # Diagnostic: check all packaging files exist before wheel build
  RUN ls -l README.md HISTORY.rst LICENSE.txt utime/version.py
  # Early check for packaging errors
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py check > /tmp/pip_utime_check.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_check.log && exit 1)
  # Install U-Time package, and show pip install log if it fails
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel > /tmp/pip_utime_build.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log && exit 1)
  # Show wheel build log immediately after attempting the wheel build
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_build.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log || true
  # Diagnostic: list contents of dist directory
  RUN echo "=== dist directory contents ===" && ls -l dist || true
  # Fail early and show build log if wheel is missing
  RUN if ! ls dist/*.whl 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "=== dist/*.whl missing ==="; cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log; exit 1; fi
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install dist/*.whl > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
--- End of Iteration 18 ---

  #25 0.304         (for example by using `find_namespace_packages(...)`/`find_namespace:`
  #25 0.304         instead of `find_packages(...)`/`find:`).
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304         You can read more about "package discovery" on setuptools documentation page:
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304         - https://setuptools.pypa.io/en/latest/userguide/package_discovery.html
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304         If you don't want 'utime.bin.defaults.utime' to be distributed and are
  #25 0.304         already explicitly excluding 'utime.bin.defaults.utime' via
  #25 0.304         `find_namespace_packages(...)/find_namespace` or `find_packages(...)/find`,
  #25 0.304         you can try to use `exclude_package_data`, or `include-package-data=False` in
  #25 0.304         combination with a more fine grained `package-data` configuration.
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304         You can read more about "package data files" on setuptools documentation page:
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304         - https://setuptools.pypa.io/en/latest/userguide/datafiles.html
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304         [^1]: For Python, any directory (with suitable naming) can be imported,
  #25 0.304               even if it does not contain any `.py` files.
  #25 0.304               On the other hand, currently there is no concept of package data
  #25 0.304               directory, all directories are treated like packages.
  #25 0.304         ********************************************************************************
  #25 0.304 
  #25 0.304 !!
  #25 0.304   check.warn(importable)

  #25 0.304         to the `packages` configuration field.

DOCKERFILE:
  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  # Install system dependencies
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  # Set locale
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  # Install Miniconda
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  # Update conda and configure channels
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  # Clone U-Time repository
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  # Create conda environment
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic step: list installed packages
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  # Diagnostic step: show requirements.txt
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  # Upgrade pip in the environment before using it
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  # Install setuptools-git and wheel explicitly to support setup.py
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3" wheel
  # Install requirements.txt one at a time for better diagnostics
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  # Ensure setuptools, wheel, and setuptools-git are up to date before package install
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel setuptools-git
  # Diagnostic: check all packaging files exist before wheel build
  RUN ls -l README.md HISTORY.rst LICENSE.txt utime/version.py
  # Diagnostic: check setup.py install_requires and setup_requires
  RUN grep "install_requires" setup.py || true
  RUN grep "setup_requires" setup.py || true
  # Early check for packaging errors
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py check > /tmp/pip_utime_check.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_check.log && exit 1)
  # Build wheel and capture log, show log immediately after
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel > /tmp/pip_utime_build.log 2>&1 || (echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_build.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log && exit 1)
  # Show wheel build log immediately after attempting the wheel build
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_build.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log || true
  # Diagnostic: list contents of dist directory
  RUN echo "=== dist directory contents ===" && ls -l dist || true
  # Fail early and show build log if wheel is missing
  RUN if ! ls dist/*.whl 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "=== dist/*.whl missing ==="; cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log; exit 1; fi
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install dist/*.whl > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  # Show pip install log for diagnostics
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  # Test import
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  # Ensure entry point is available
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  # Set working directory to root of repo
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

  - **CPU Architecture:** Confirm all conda/pip packages are available for x86_64/amd64 (should be default for your setup).
  - **Operating System:** All instructions are Linux-compatible (e.g., Ubuntu 20.04) and work in Docker containers based on python:3.10-slim or ubuntu:20.04 images.
  - **No GPU:** Do NOT attempt to install or configure CUDA/cuDNN or GPU-enabled TensorFlow/PyTorch. Use CPU-only TensorFlow (or allow conda/pip to select the default).
  - **Memory & Storage:** Ensure sufficient RAM (at least 8 GB recommended; more for large datasets) and disk space for datasets and checkpoints.
  - **Development Tools:** Docker 28.1.1 features are supported; ensure COPY and WORKDIR in Dockerfiles are set appropriately (e.g., context /home/cc/EnvGym/data/20260501_153628_tensorblock_gpt-4.1/U-Time).

  FROM ubuntu:20.04
  ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  RUN apt-get update && \
      apt-get install -y \
          wget \
          git \
          build-essential \
          libgl1-mesa-glx \
          libglib2.0-0 \
          libx11-dev \
          libtk8.6 \
          bzip2 \
          ca-certificates \
          sudo \
          locales \
          && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  RUN locale-gen en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
  ENV LANGUAGE en_US:en
  ENV LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
  ENV CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda
  RUN wget --quiet https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh -O /tmp/miniconda.sh && \
      bash /tmp/miniconda.sh -b -p $CONDA_DIR && \
      rm /tmp/miniconda.sh
  ENV PATH=$CONDA_DIR/bin:$PATH
  RUN conda update -n base -c defaults conda && \
      conda config --add channels conda-forge && \
      conda config --add channels defaults && \
      conda config --set channel_priority flexible
  WORKDIR /root
  RUN git clone https://github.com/perslev/U-Time.git
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  RUN set -e; \
      conda env create --name u-sleep --file environment.yaml > /tmp/conda_env_create.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/conda_env_create.log && exit 1)
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep conda list
  RUN echo "=== /root/U-Time/requirements.txt ===" && cat /root/U-Time/requirements.txt
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade pip
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install "setuptools-git>=0.3" wheel
  RUN bash -c '\
      touch /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
      while read req; do \
          if [ ! -z "$req" ] && [ "${req:0:1}" != "#" ]; then \
              echo "=== Installing: $req ===" | tee -a /tmp/pip_individual_install.log; \
              conda run -n u-sleep pip install --no-cache-dir "$req" >> /tmp/pip_individual_install.log 2>&1 || \
                  (echo "=== pip install failed for: $req ===" && tail -n 40 /tmp/pip_individual_install.log && exit 1); \
          fi; \
      done < requirements.txt'
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_individual_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_individual_install.log
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel setuptools-git
  RUN ls -l README.md HISTORY.rst LICENSE.txt utime/version.py
  RUN grep "install_requires" setup.py || true
  RUN grep "setup_requires" setup.py || true
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py check > /tmp/pip_utime_check.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_check.log && exit 1)
  # Diagnostic: Show version file before build
  RUN echo "=== utime/version.py ===" && cat utime/version.py
  # Diagnostic: Show MANIFEST.in
  RUN echo "=== MANIFEST.in ===" && cat MANIFEST.in
  # Diagnostic: Show current git HEAD and status
  RUN git rev-parse HEAD
  RUN git status
  # Ensure .git exists and is readable
  RUN test -d .git && echo ".git directory present" || (echo ".git directory missing" && exit 1)
  # Show setup.py version string extraction for debug
  RUN grep version setup.py || true
  # Build wheel and capture log, show log immediately after
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel > /tmp/pip_utime_build.log 2>&1 || (echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_build.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log && exit 1)
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_build.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log || true
  RUN echo "=== dist directory contents ===" && ls -l dist || true
  RUN if ! ls dist/*.whl 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "=== dist/*.whl missing ==="; cat /tmp/pip_utime_build.log; exit 1; fi
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep pip install dist/*.whl > /tmp/pip_utime_install.log 2>&1 || (cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log && exit 1)
  RUN echo "=== /tmp/pip_utime_install.log ===" && cat /tmp/pip_utime_install.log || true
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep python -c "import utime"
  RUN conda run -n u-sleep ut --help || true
  WORKDIR /root/U-Time
  ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
